feedback.text: (Principality of Sealand) As a Sealander, its exceptionally rare that I get to participate or have my voice heard on the European stage, and given such an opportunity, I would like to voice my support behind the Stop Killing Games initiative. My primary concern is that of erosion of consumer rights. Owning an interactive piece of software used to entail full rights and use of the product paid for. This has been eroded to where purchase at full price ultimately results in a piece of unusable media beyond the efforts or abilities of the users. A good example is Google Stadia, which shut down access to hundreds of games that were fully paid for by users. This level of erosion of consumer protection is unacceptable. This initiative can ensure publishers take due diligence and are able to provide end users any necessary information for continuing use of the software after end of the products life.
